channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Andy Burnham signals limited flexibility on tax policy ahead of leadership transition
item.description: Andy Burnham, who is expected to become UK Prime Minister later this month following his bid to lead the Labour Party, has indicated there is limited scope for adjustments to tax policy while maintain...

item.title: Scottish Beach Closed to Polluted Stream Water Contact Due to Contamination Warning
item.description: Authorities in Scotland have issued a public warning advising visitors to avoid contact with water from a polluted burn flowing into a beach area. The Scottish Environment Protection Agency (SEPA) has...

item.title: Chhattisgarh High Court clarifies that government school students cannot be forced to recite religious prayers
item.description: The Chhattisgarh High Court has clarified that students studying in government schools cannot be compelled to recite Hindu prayers or any religious hymns, reinforcing the constitutional principle of s...

item.title: Tanzania’s Martin Chegere promoted to Associate Professor at age 39 after strong academic performance
item.description: Martin Chegere, a Senior Lecturer at the University of Dar es Salaam (UDSM) in Tanzania, has been promoted to the rank of Associate Professor at the age of 39, marking a significant milestone in his a...

item.title: Tanzania Revenue Authority marks 30 years of growth, digital transformation and expanding tax base
item.description: The Tanzania Revenue Authority (TRA) has marked 30 years since its establishment, reflecting a major transformation in the country’s tax administration and revenue collection systems. When it started ...

item.title: Passenger bus accident near Balochistan–KP border leaves 40 dead, 8 injured
item.description: At least 40 people lost their lives and eight others were injured when a passenger bus travelling towards Peshawar fell into a deep ravine near the border area between Balochistan’s Sherani district a...

item.title: Riyad Mahrez Announces International Retirement After Algeria’s World Cup Exit
item.description: Riyad Mahrez has officially announced his retirement from international football following Algeria’s 2-0 defeat to Switzerland in the Round of 32 at the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The match, played in Vanco...

item.title: Fresh legal developments in Yanson family dispute emerge during patriarch’s 97th birthday commemoration
item.description: A new round of legal and family conflict has surfaced within the prominent Yanson family of Negros Occidental during the commemoration of the late patriarch Ricardo Yanson Sr.’s 97th birthday. The eve...

item.title: Federal appeals court requires bond hearings for migrants detained over 90 days by ICE
item.description: A U.S. federal appeals court ruled in a 2-1 decision that Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) cannot detain migrants for more than 90 days without providing them an opportunity for a bond hearin...

item.title: Rethinking work after redundancy: balancing financial stability and personal meaning
item.description: The article explores the emotional dilemma faced by a person re-entering the workforce after six months of unemployment following redundancy. During their time away from work, they embraced a slower l...

item.title: Why King Charles’ change of ‘Defender of the Faith’ title is rooted in historical precedent
item.description: In this opinion column, Tom Eaton examines the controversy surrounding King Charles’ reported decision to alter the traditional royal title ‘Defender of the Faith’ to ‘Protector of the Space for Faith...

item.title: Halsey expresses admiration for Nick Cave during Open’er Festival performance
item.description: Halsey expressed admiration for Nick Cave during their performance at the Open’er Festival 2026 in Poland, where both artists appeared on the same day’s line-up. The festival opened on 1 July with per...

item.title: South Africa Women knocked out of T20 World Cup after batting collapse against England
item.description: South Africa’s women’s cricket team, the Proteas, were eliminated from the T20 World Cup after a 40-run defeat to England in the semi-final at The Oval. Chasing a target of 170, South Africa struggled...

item.title: Zuma’s renewed links with the Gupta family revive South Africa’s state capture debate
item.description: Nearly a decade after the state capture scandal shook South Africa and ended Jacob Zuma’s presidency, fresh controversy has emerged following reports and photographs showing Zuma reunited with Ajay Gu...

item.title: Marcos expresses gratitude to Canada for support of international law in South China Sea
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. expressed gratitude to Canada for its continued support of a rules-based international order in the South China Sea during a bilateral engagement in Vancouver with Canad...

item.title: Kio Amachree’s Allegations on UK Involvement in Nigerian Corruption and Financial Flows
item.description: This opinion piece by Donu Kogbara focuses on the strong criticisms made by Kio Amachree, a diaspora activist and president of a Stockholm-based accountability platform called Worldview International....

item.title: Australia’s potential World Cup knockout path if they defeat Egypt
item.description: Australia is preparing for a crucial FIFA World Cup knockout match against Egypt in the Round of 32, with kick-off scheduled for Saturday at 4am AEST in Dallas. Both teams enter the match with closely...

item.title: Switzerland defeat Algeria 2–0 to reach World Cup round of 16
item.description: Switzerland secured a 2–0 victory over Algeria in their World Cup match on Thursday, confirming their place in the round of 16. The Swiss side was led by goals from Breel Embolo and Dan Ndoye in a dis...

item.title: Five arrested in Lahore over alleged kidnapping and assault of two foreign nationals
item.description: Five suspects have been arrested in Lahore following serious allegations of kidnapping, extortion, and sexual assault involving two foreign women. According to a First Information Report (FIR) registe...

item.title: Tinubu says economic reforms are stabilising Nigeria and attracting investor confidence
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has reiterated his defence of the economic reforms introduced by his administration, insisting that the policies are beginning to produce measurable positive outcomes for Nigeria...
